steps.title: Explain the abrupt ending
steps.content: The underlined portion first names a possible flaw: Master and Commander has a rather abrupt ending. It then immediately reduces the force of that objection by explaining that Post Captain continues neatly from the same point.

steps.title: Reject unsupported functions
steps.content: Choice A discusses which readers would most enjoy Post Captain, but the passage never makes a claim about reader preference. Choice B relies on the later comparison to The Forsyte Saga, not on the underlined sentence itself. Choice D invents a claim that these are two of O'Brian's most engaging novels. The underlined detail explains why the abrupt ending is not necessarily a flaw. The answer is C.
